mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods despite rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict return sign changes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both series trend upward together.
failure_condition: Joint upward trends in retail sales and housing starts coincide with negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases or the signal reverses direction in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above 0.4 percent coincide with positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances or central bank response remains accommodative despite threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll gains and unemployment declines occur simultaneously with negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases or the divergence metric shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops below -0.5 percent are followed by positive 20-day returns in >70% of instances or the threshold fails to separate return distributions.
